inst_welcome_text_1:
Welcome to this experiment. 
 
 Please read the following instructions carefully.
inst_text_ver_1: In the next screen, you will see two dots jumping up and down
inst_text_hor_1: Now they will jump from left to right.
inst_text_amb_1:
If they jump at different times, you can see either up-down or left-right motion. 
 
 Please pay attention in which direction the dots are jumping in the next display.
inst_text_amb_2:
You likely saw them jumping up and down or from left to right. 
 
 Both are possible
inst_text_amb_3: Whether you see up-down motion or left-right motion can be influenced by priming either motion.
inst_text_amb_4: In the next screen, the prime will likely make you see up-down motion for a while.
inst_text_amb_5: Now, the prime will likely make you see left-right motion for a while.
inst_text_amb_6: Although the prime will influence you to see either motion, the direction might still switch at some point in the later, ambiguous phase.
inst_text_amb_7:
Now you will see the dots for a while, with an initial prime to see left-right motion. 
 
 Pay attention if you continuously see left-right motion or if it switches to up-down motion at some point.
inst_text_amb_8:
You will see the dots again for a while, but this time primed to up-down motion. 
 
 Pay attention if you continuously see up-down motion or if it switches to left-right motion at some point.
inst_text_amb_9:
You might have noticed a change in direction at some point during the previous trials. 
 
 If you did not notice a change, this is also fine.
inst_text_amb_10: A switch can happen, but does not necessarily occur.
inst_text_mml_1: In the next screen, the distance of the dots will vary over time, making it easier to see a change in the direction of motion.
inst_text_mml_2: If the spacing is very narrow at first, you will likely see left-right motion at first and up-down motion later.
inst_text_mml_3: If the spacing is very wide at first, you will likely see up-down motion at first and left-right motion later.
inst_text_mml_4: Next, please press SPACE as soon as you notice a change in the direction of movement.
inst_text_mml_5:
There are now a few trials with changing distances. 
 
 Please press SPACE as soon as you notice a change in the direction of movement.
exp_text_1: From now on, focus on the center of the screen and try to keep your gaze on the fixation cross.
exp_text_2: You will now see three of the dot pairs in a row.
exp_text_3: You might notice how they all do the same thing - they either all go up-down or all go left-right
exp_text_4: As before, how you initially see the dots jumping can be influenced by a prime.
exp_text_5: In the next screen you will likely see all dots move up and down for a while.
exp_text_6: Now you will likely see them move from left to right.
exp_text_7: As you maybe noticed, after the prime (first few jumps), the dots will likely jump in the direction indicated by the prime.
exp_text_8:
This might change after a while. 
 
 If the dots are primed to jump up and down, you will probably see them jumping up and down for a while, but it might switch to left right motion later.
exp_text_9:
The same goes the other way around. 
 
 If the dots are primed to jump from left to right, you will probably see them jumping from left to right for a while, but it might switch to up-down motion later.
exp_text_10: Please only pay attention to the pair of dots in the CENTER.
exp_text_11: If you notice the direction change, press SPACE as fast as possible.
exp_text_12:
For example: 
 
 The dots might be primed to jump up and down. 
 
 If in the later part the direction of the center pair of dots changes from up-down motion to left-right motion, press SPACE.
exp_text_13:
The same way, the dots might be primed to jump from left to right. 
 
 If in the later part the direction of the center pair of dots changes from left-right motion to up-down motion, press SPACE.
exp_text_14:
You might notice a manipulation in the left or right pair of dots. 
 
 Do NOT respond to this. 
 
 Only press space if you notice a change in direction in the center pair of dots.
exp_text_15:
Not all trials will have a prime or a manipulation. 
 
 Still, if you notice a change in the direction of the center pair of dots, press SPACE
exp_text_16: Do not press SPACE if you do not see a change.
exp_text_17: When you are ready, press SPACE to start.
exp_break_text_1:
Time for a short break. 
 Press space to continue when you are ready.
exp_complete_text:
Thank you for participating! 
 Please press SPACE to finish and inform the experimenter about the completion.
